pattern: To maximize one entry of an array whose neighbors may differ by at most one and whose total is capped, you binary search the peak height and, for each candidate, compute the cheapest mountain-shaped fill on either side with a closed-form arithmetic-series sum.
hints: Fix a target value v for the chosen index; the cheapest valid array makes every other entry as small as possible, decreasing by one step away from the peak until it hits the floor of one.

For a side with a given number of slots, the minimal sum is an arithmetic series that decreases from v-1 down to either v minus the slot count or to one, whichever it reaches first; derive a constant-time formula for it.
The minimal total sum is monotonic in v, so binary search the largest v whose left-side plus right-side plus v stays within maxSum.

edge_inputs.description: single element gets the whole budget
every entry forced to one when budget equals n
index at the left end so only the right side absorbs slope
index at the right end mirrors the left-end case
large array with generous budget so the peak grows well above one

stress.description: small n so the loop-based oracle stays fast, with index and budget within bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 800)))
    index = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    maxSum = rng.randint(n, n + 1000000)
    return [n, index, maxSum]
stress.note: maxSum >= n keeps the instance feasible

stress.description: tight budgets near n to stress the all-ones region of the formula
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 800)))
    index = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    maxSum = rng.randint(n, n + n)
    return [n, index, maxSum]
stress.note: small slope budgets exercise the floor-at-one branch

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maxValue(self, n, index, maxSum):
        def side_cost(slots, peak):
            # minimal sum of `slots` entries decreasing from peak-1, floored at 1
            total = 0
            cur = peak - 1
            for _ in range(slots):
                v = cur if cur >= 1 else 1
                total += v
                cur -= 1
            return total
        left = index
        right = n - 1 - index
        lo, hi = 1, maxSum
        ans = 1
        while lo <= hi:
            mid = (lo + hi) // 2
            total = mid + side_cost(left, mid) + side_cost(right, mid)
            if total <= maxSum:
                ans = mid
                lo = mid + 1
            else:
                hi = mid - 1
        return ans
